mapping.a_element: total subjective risk being split into two additive parts
mapping.b_element: total population complexity (CWMMSE) being split into two additive parts

brief_justification: The only real overlap is the generic pattern of decomposing an information-theoretic scalar into two additive components; the driving machinery differs. Paper A derives its terms from a strictly-proper-loss / proper-scoring-rule (Bregman) decomposition of a subjective risk in a learning-theoretic setting, where epistemic means reducible knowledge uncertainty, whereas Paper B builds a clustering-weighted sample-entropy of a trajectory ensemble with a plug-in estimator, splitting within-trajectory complexity from genuine (non-reducible) population diversity, so no role-for-role correspondence of the actual mechanisms holds.
